title: The geographic starting point of the chronicle
summary: Shanxi establishes the birthplace. The chapter sets a tone of precision before the academic acceleration begins.

title: The first high-acceleration academic anchor
summary: Entering USTC in 1980 at age 16 for a Physics major is the first high-velocity milestone. It establishes the intellectual momentum that carries through the doctoral years and beyond.

title: Treat the doctorate as the first stable anchor, not a decorative credential
summary: The doctorate is the strongest pre-company anchor — the first place where the reader feels solid ground under the biography. The technical depth of the Ph.D. makes the later persistence in encryption, trust infrastructure, and enterprise security legible.

title: The hinge between research depth and company formation
summary: The California chapter establishes a U.S. research context before the company narrative begins — the bridge between Hong Kong academia and Silicon Valley company-building.

title: Anchor the founder story in dated releases, then show how the company thickens around them
summary: The 2013 OpenStack and VMware release trail, followed by the 2014 advisory-board signal, makes Bloombase legible as an emerging institution in a hard trust market — named role, dated publications, infrastructure-market language, then a broader company-building signal a year later.

title: Read the middle years as an accumulation of trust surfaces, not a logo parade
summary: Certification, alliance status, HSM interoperability, security validation, storage-networking proof, and cloud-marketplace distribution accumulate across different years and different parts of the stack. Each artifact marks a different kind of institutional trust — certification, HSM, infrastructure, cloud distribution — progression, not repetition.

eyebrow: Deep brief 07 · Geography with a reason
title: Use geography only where place changes the strategic reading of the story
summary: The Launch Academy profile explains why Canada mattered strategically, while the company offices page confirms the move belongs to a real operating footprint. Each location change in this chronicle carries a strategic rationale paired with operating-footprint proof.

title: The parallel academic arc: Founding Dean of AI and Institute co-founder
summary: From 2018 to 2023, Sean Xiang held senior academic and institutional leadership roles in Shenzhen — Founding Dean of the School of Artificial Intelligence at Shenzhen Technology University, then Chair Professor and Co-founder at the X-Institute of Shenzhen — all while leading Bloombase. The academic roles and the company arc are parallel, not sequential.

title: Close on AI infrastructure with evidence of reframing, visibility, and alliance execution
summary: The 2023 NVIDIA argument reframes the technical thesis, the public news surface shows the company speaking, and the 2025 Utimaco announcement names the AI-era alliance directly. The final chapter reads as a current infrastructure thesis with dated, auditable signals.
